title: baccharis serrulata var. pingraea
intro: Baccharis serrulata var. pingraea, also known as Mexican silvermist or purple false heather, is a shrub native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Baccharis serrulata var. pingraea include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and promote digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
